evidence_refs.source_quote: Date March 12, 2026
The Report compiles the MTA Mission Statement, Stakeholder Assessment, and the MTA goals and associated performance measurements from 2025.
Metropolitan Transportation Authority Mission Statement, Measurements, and Performance Indicators Report Covering Fiscal Year 2025
Delivering results for all New Yorkers
2025 Annual Report

source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) preserves and enhances the quality of life and economic health of the region it serves through the cost-efficient provision of safe, on-time, reliable, and clean transportation services.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation agency, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA) preserves and enhances the quality of life and economic health of the region it serves through the cost-efficient provision of safe, on-time, reliable, and clean transportation services.
The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation agency, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: In 2025, the MTA completed one of its most ambitious service overhauls with the full rollout of a new bus network for Queens

payload.description: Full rollout of a new bus network for Queens, launched in two phases over summer 2025, added 11 new routes, expanded overnight service, improved transit access for 68,000 more residents

evidence_refs.source_quote: In 2025, the MTA completed one of its most ambitious service overhauls with the full rollout of a new bus network for Queens
124 total bus routes
$33.7 M additional annual investment in operations

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA advanced the Second Avenue Subway Phase 2... secured the largest tunneling contract award in MTA history. Early work began at the end of 2025, with major construction to begin in 2026 and tunneling in 2027.

payload.status: early work began end of 2025, major construction 2026, tunneling 2027
payload.description: Extends the Q from East 96 to 125 Streets, will serve 100,000 daily riders

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA advanced the Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 and the Interborough Express projects. The Second Avenue Subway extension secured the largest tunneling contract award in MTA history. Early work began at the end of 2025, with major construction to begin in 2026 and tunneling in 2027. Phase 2, which extends the from East 96 to 125 Streets, will serve 100,000 daily riders.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Interborough Express
tool_args.raw_text: Design and engineering began on the Interborough Express in August, supported by a $2.75 billion investment from the 2025–2029 Capital Plan.

payload.description: Transit link serving historically underserved communities in Brooklyn and Queens, connecting them to subway, bus, and LIRR, serving 900,000 residents

evidence_refs.source_quote: Design and engineering began on the Interborough Express in August, supported by a $2.75 billion investment from the 2025–2029 Capital Plan.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Phase 1
tool_args.raw_text: In October, the MTA completed Phase 1 of the Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement.

payload.description: Replaced 8,240 feet of track and 128 bridge spans on the 132-year-old structure from East 115 to East 123 Street in East Harlem, completed 51 months ahead of schedule and $93 million under budget

evidence_refs.source_quote: In October, the MTA completed Phase 1 of the Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement. Stretching from East 115 to East 123 Street in East Harlem, the work replaced 8,240 feet of track and 128 bridge spans on the 132-year-old structure that carries nearly all Metro-North trains into Manhattan. Completed 51 months ahead of schedule and $93 million under budget

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: Railcar Acceptance and Testing Facility
tool_args.raw_text: the MTA celebrated the opening of its first-ever Railcar Acceptance and Testing Facility in Brooklyn

payload.description: First-of-its-kind facility to test and process more than 1,500 new subway cars, delivered on time and $5.5 million under budget

evidence_refs.source_quote: the MTA celebrated the opening of its first-ever Railcar Acceptance and Testing Facility in Brooklyn, a major milestone in modernizing the subway system. Delivered on time and $5.5 million under budget, the facility will test and process more than 1,500 new subway cars

tool_args.label: LIRR Valley Stream Station Renovation
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA completed major renovations to the LIRR Valley Stream Station in December.

payload.description: Major renovations including new escalator, staircase, elevator, parking spaces, and CCTV cameras, plus improved lighting, sidewalks, and crosswalks. Completed on budget and on schedule.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA completed major renovations to the LIRR Valley Stream Station in December. Completed on budget and on schedule, the more modern station is now in a state of good repair. Valley Stream has a new escalator, staircase, elevator, parking spaces, and CCTV cameras, plus improved lighting, sidewalks, and crosswalks.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

tool_args.label: 2025-2029 Capital Program
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA Board approved the largest-ever Capital Program for 2025 through 2029. It invests $68.4 billion over five years

payload.description: Largest state of good repair investment in MTA history, investing $68.4 billion over five years to rebuild, improve, and expand the MTA network. Includes purchasing 2,000 new railcars, making more than 60 stations accessible, modernizing 75 miles of signals, installing modern fare gates at 150 locations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA Board approved the largest-ever Capital Program for 2025 through 2029. It invests $68.4 billion over five years to rebuild, improve, and expand the MTA network
